description: NAT Gateway S3 Traffic Detection — Identifies EC2 instances routing S3 traffic through a paid NAT Gateway when a free S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint should be used instead. Also detects existing VPC endpoints configured for the wrong region.

cost.note: NAT Gateway charges $0.045/hr (fixed) + $0.045/GB of data processed. S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint is completely FREE — no hourly charge, no data processing charge. Replacing NAT with Gateway Endpoint eliminates 100% of the per-GB NAT processing cost for S3 traffic.

severity_rules.name: s3_traffic_via_nat
severity_rules.condition: nat_bytes_out_mb_per_hr_avg > nat_bytes_out_mb_per_hr_min AND (no aws_vpc_endpoint for same-region S3 exists OR existing endpoint service_name region != provider region)
severity_rules.description: EC2 instances are sending traffic to S3 through a NAT Gateway, incurring $0.045/GB data processing fees. An S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint routes this traffic directly within AWS's network at zero cost. If an existing aws_vpc_endpoint is present but its service_name references a different region than the provider, it is ineffective and the correct-region endpoint must be added.
